{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 18:28, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{Cite sources|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}} * {{ill|Yoshimura There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|こうちけんごこくじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|こうちけんごこくじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 18:28, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{Cite sources|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM== See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|こうちけんごこくじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 18:28, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{Cite sources|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|こうちけんごこくじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|こうちけんごこくじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}} * {{ill|Yoshimura {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 18:28, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{Cite sources|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }}[[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|こうちけんごこくじんじゃ}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kochi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kochi City]] [[Category:History of Kochi City]] [[Category:Jinshōtai]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 18:28, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{Cite sources|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=draft}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件|12=draft}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範|12=draft}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=draft}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=draft}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|こうちけんごこくじんじゃ}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine]] {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine]] }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 18:28, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{Cite sources|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=draft}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件|12=draft}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範|12=draft}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=draft}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=draft}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|こうちけんごこくじんじゃ}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine]] }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 18:28, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{Cite sources|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=draft}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件|12=draft}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範|12=draft}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=draft}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=draft}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|こうちけんごこくじんじゃ}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine]] }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=draft}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件|12=draft}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範|12=draft}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=draft}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=draft}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|こうちけんごこくじんじゃ}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine]] }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=draft}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件|12=draft}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範|12=draft}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=draft}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=draft}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|こうちけんごこくじんじゃ}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine]] They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=draft}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件|12=draft}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範|12=draft}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=draft}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakaoka In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill||Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=draft}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai Incident|ja|堺事件|12=draft}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyoshige|ja|山内豊範|12=draft}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=draft}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=draft}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takechi Zuizan|ja|武市瑞山|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryoma|ja|坂本龍馬|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintaro|ja|中岡慎太郎|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=draft}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|こうちけんごこくじんじゃ}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine {{citation needed|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine]]
