(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} }} {{ill|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: [[:en:Takechi Hanpeita|Takechi Zuizan]] (Takechi Hanpeita), [[:en:Sakamoto Ryōma|Sakamoto Ryoma]], [[:en:Nakaoka Shintarō|Nakaoka Shintaro]], and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the [[:en:Sakai incident|Sakai Incident]] (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord [[:en:Yamauchi Toyonori|Yamauchi Toyoshige]] held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Chidokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]] * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}} * [[:en:Takechi Hanpeita|Takechi Zuizan]] * [[:en:Sakamoto Ryōma|Sakamoto Ryoma]] * [[:en:Nakaoka Shintarō|Nakaoka Shintaro]] * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence [[commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11672548]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. (Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} }} {{ill|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: [[:en:Takechi Hanpeita|Takechi Zuizan]] (Takechi Hanpeita), [[:en:Sakamoto Ryōma|Sakamoto Ryoma]], [[:en:Nakaoka Shintarō|Nakaoka Shintaro]], and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the [[:en:Sakai incident|Sakai Incident]] (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord [[:en:Yamauchi Toyonori|Yamauchi Toyoshige]] held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Chidokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]] * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}} * [[:en:Takechi Hanpeita|Takechi Zuizan]] * [[:en:Sakamoto Ryōma|Sakamoto Ryoma]] * [[:en:Nakaoka Shintarō|Nakaoka Shintaro]] * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based [[commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11672548]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 18:28, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|33.544583|N|133.571111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} }} {{ill|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (高知縣護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: [[:en:Takechi Hanpeita|Takechi Zuizan]] (Takechi Hanpeita), [[:en:Sakamoto Ryōma|Sakamoto Ryoma]], [[:en:Nakaoka Shintarō|Nakaoka Shintaro]], and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the [[:en:Sakai incident|Sakai Incident]] (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord [[:en:Yamauchi Toyonori|Yamauchi Toyoshige]] held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Chidokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]] * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}} * [[:en:Takechi Hanpeita|Takechi Zuizan]] * [[:en:Sakamoto Ryōma|Sakamoto Ryoma]] * [[:en:Nakaoka Shintarō|Nakaoka Shintaro]] * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] * [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]] * [[co:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11672548]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=does not appear to be notable[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 18:28, 1 November 2023 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|33.544583|N|133.571111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} }} {{ill|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (高知縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Hanpeita|en|Takechi Hanpeita|WD=Q1074298|lt=Takechi Zuizan}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryōma|en|Sakamoto Ryōma|WD=Q378450|lt=Sakamoto Ryoma}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintarō|en|Nakaoka Shintarō|WD=Q3138626|lt=Nakaoka Shintaro}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai incident|en|Sakai incident|WD=Q1030084|lt=Sakai Incident}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyonori|en|Yamauchi Toyonori|WD=Q11466487|lt=Yamauchi Toyoshige}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Chidokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening [[co:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|33.544583|N|133.571111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} }} {{ill|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (高知縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Hanpeita|en|Takechi Hanpeita|WD=Q1074298|lt=Takechi Zuizan}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryōma|en|Sakamoto Ryōma|WD=Q378450|lt=Sakamoto Ryoma}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintarō|en|Nakaoka Shintarō|WD=Q3138626|lt=Nakaoka Shintaro}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai incident|en|Sakai incident|WD=Q1030084|lt=Sakai Incident}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyonori|en|Yamauchi Toyonori|WD=Q11466487|lt=Yamauchi Toyoshige}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Chidokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|WD=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|WD=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|WD=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|WD=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult}} * {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}} * {{ill|Takechi Hanpeita|en|Takechi Hanpeita|WD=Q1074298|lt=Takechi Zuizan}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryōma|en|Sakamoto Ryōma|WD=Q378450|lt=Sakamoto Ryoma}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintarō|en|Nakaoka Shintarō|WD=Q3138626|lt=Nakaoka Shintaro}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}} == References == {{reflist}} Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|33.544583|N|133.571111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251}} }} {{ill|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (高知縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Hanpeita|en|Takechi Hanpeita|WD=Q1074298|lt=Takechi Zuizan}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryōma|en|Sakamoto Ryōma|WD=Q378450|lt=Sakamoto Ryoma}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintarō|en|Nakaoka Shintarō|WD=Q3138626|lt=Nakaoka Shintaro}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai incident|en|Sakai incident|WD=Q1030084|lt=Sakai Incident}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyonori|en|Yamauchi Toyonori|WD=Q11466487|lt=Yamauchi Toyoshige}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Chidokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|WD=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|WD=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|WD=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|WD=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult}} * {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}} * {{ill|Takechi Hanpeita|en|Takechi Hanpeita|WD=Q1074298|lt=Takechi Zuizan}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryōma|en|Sakamoto Ryōma|WD=Q378450|lt=Sakamoto Ryoma}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintarō|en|Nakaoka Shintarō|WD=Q3138626|lt=Nakaoka Shintaro}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}} == References == {{reflist}} (Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} }} {{ill|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (高知縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Hanpeita|en|Takechi Hanpeita|WD=Q1074298|lt=Takechi Zuizan|1=Takechi Hanpeita}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryōma|en|Sakamoto Ryōma|WD=Q378450|lt=Sakamoto Ryoma|1=Sakamoto Ryōma}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintarō|en|Nakaoka Shintarō|WD=Q3138626|lt=Nakaoka Shintaro|1=Nakaoka Shintarō}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai incident|en|Sakai incident|WD=Q1030084|lt=Sakai Incident|1=Sakai incident}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyonori|en|Yamauchi Toyonori|WD=Q11466487|lt=Yamauchi Toyoshige|1=Yamauchi Toyonori}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Chidokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * [[co:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|33.544583|N|133.571111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} }} {{ill|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (高知縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Hanpeita|en|Takechi Hanpeita|WD=Q1074298|lt=Takechi Zuizan|1=Takechi Hanpeita}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryōma|en|Sakamoto Ryōma|WD=Q378450|lt=Sakamoto Ryoma|1=Sakamoto Ryōma}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintarō|en|Nakaoka Shintarō|WD=Q3138626|lt=Nakaoka Shintaro|1=Nakaoka Shintarō}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai incident|en|Sakai incident|WD=Q1030084|lt=Sakai Incident|1=Sakai incident}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyonori|en|Yamauchi Toyonori|WD=Q11466487|lt=Yamauchi Toyoshige|1=Yamauchi Toyonori}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Chidokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple|1=Martial temple}} and (Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} }} {{ill|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (高知縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Hanpeita|en|Takechi Hanpeita|WD=Q1074298|lt=Takechi Zuizan|1=Takechi Hanpeita}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryōma|en|Sakamoto Ryōma|WD=Q378450|lt=Sakamoto Ryoma|1=Sakamoto Ryōma}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintarō|en|Nakaoka Shintarō|WD=Q3138626|lt=Nakaoka Shintaro|1=Nakaoka Shintarō}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai incident|en|Sakai incident|WD=Q1030084|lt=Sakai Incident|1=Sakai incident}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyonori|en|Yamauchi Toyonori|WD=Q11466487|lt=Yamauchi Toyoshige|1=Yamauchi Toyonori}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Chidokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple|1=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple|1=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|1=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance|1=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|WD=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden|1=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|WD=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine|1=Walhalla (memorial)}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|WD=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds|1=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|WD=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult|1=Greek hero cult}} * {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}} * {{ill|Takechi Hanpeita|en|Takechi Hanpeita|WD=Q1074298|lt=Takechi Zuizan|1=Takechi Hanpeita}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryōma|en|Sakamoto Ryōma|WD=Q378450|lt=Sakamoto Ryoma|1=Sakamoto Ryōma}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintarō|en|Nakaoka Shintarō|WD=Q3138626|lt=Nakaoka Shintaro|1=Nakaoka Shintarō}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|高知縣護國神社|version=102013859|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{everybodywiki link|Kochi Gokoku Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q11672548}} [[Category:1869 establishments]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Kochi, Kochi]] [[Category:Jinshōtai]] [[Category:Kōchi, Kōchi]] [[Category:Military monuments and memorials]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * [[co:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|33.544583|N|133.571111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Kochi|WD=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} }} {{ill|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (高知縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638349}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Hanpeita|en|Takechi Hanpeita|WD=Q1074298|lt=Takechi Zuizan|1=Takechi Hanpeita}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryōma|en|Sakamoto Ryōma|WD=Q378450|lt=Sakamoto Ryoma|1=Sakamoto Ryōma}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintarō|en|Nakaoka Shintarō|WD=Q3138626|lt=Nakaoka Shintaro|1=Nakaoka Shintarō}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413307}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai incident|en|Sakai incident|WD=Q1030084|lt=Sakai Incident|1=Sakai incident}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyonori|en|Yamauchi Toyonori|WD=Q11466487|lt=Yamauchi Toyoshige|1=Yamauchi Toyonori}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Chidokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple|1=Martial temple}} and [[co:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kochi Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:高知縣護國神社]] [[ja:高知縣護國神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=August 28, 2017}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kotikengokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|33.544583|N|133.571111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Kochi|qid=Q200251|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} }} {{ill|Kochi Gokoku Shrine|ja|高知縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (高知縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|qid=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.{{Cite web |title=Kōchi Gokoku Jinja Yokaren Monument |url=https://www.kamikazeimages.net/monuments/kochi-yokaren/index.htm |access-date=2023-10-22 |website=www.kamikazeimages.net}} It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead. Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|qid=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|qid=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} It is on a hill. It enshrines over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture who died in national crises. There is a Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine Support Association. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * 105 Tosa domain samurai who died in the Boshin War during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638349}}. * Four Great Loyalists of the Meiji Restoration: {{ill|Takechi Hanpeita|en|Takechi Hanpeita|qid=Q1074298|lt=Takechi Zuizan|1=Takechi Hanpeita}} (Takechi Hanpeita), {{ill|Sakamoto Ryōma|en|Sakamoto Ryōma|qid=Q378450|lt=Sakamoto Ryoma|1=Sakamoto Ryōma}}, {{ill|Nakaoka Shintarō|en|Nakaoka Shintarō|qid=Q3138626|lt=Nakaoka Shintaro|1=Nakaoka Shintarō}}, and {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11413307}}. * 11 Tosa domain samurai who died in the {{ill|Sakai incident|en|Sakai incident|qid=Q1030084|lt=Sakai Incident|1=Sakai incident}} (Keio 4). * Tosa domain samurai and others who died in the Boshin War, Sino-Japanese War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Pacific War (Great East Asia War). * Over 41,000 war dead from Kochi Prefecture and those with connections to the prefecture. == History == In Meiji 1 (1868), the Tosa domain lord {{ill|Yamauchi Toyonori|en|Yamauchi Toyonori|qid=Q11466487|lt=Yamauchi Toyoshige|1=Yamauchi Toyonori}} held a requiem for 105 Tosa domain samurai who died during the Eastern Expedition, including members of the Jinshōtai, at the domain school {{ill|Kochi Chidokan|ja|高知致道館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kochi Chidokan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. To enshrine their spirits, a shrine was established at Oshima Cape on Godaisan Mountain, named Oshima Cape Shrine, and the shrine buildings were completed the following year in Meiji 2 (1869). On May 31, Meiji 8 (1875), it was renamed {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. In Showa 14 (1939), it was renamed Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine and designated a specific Gokoku Shrine. After World War II, it was no longer managed by the state due to the Religious Corporations Ordinance under the occupation, and its name reverted to Oshima Cape Shrine. In 1959 (Showa 34), due to the strong wishes of many worshippers, its name was restored to Kochi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a special shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Kaitaku Shrine == Major Festivals == * '''Spring Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' April 1 * '''Spring Grand Festival''' April 2 * '''Mitama Festival''' August 15 * '''Autumn Grand Festival (Evening Festival)''' November 1 * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' November 2 * '''Reisai''' every 10th, 20th, and 27th of the month * '''Memorial Day Festival''' every morning at 7 AM == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|qid=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|qid=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple|1=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|qid=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple|1=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|qid=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|1=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|qid=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|qid=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance|1=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|qid=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden|1=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|qid=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|qid=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|qid=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|qid=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine|1=Walhalla (memorial)}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|qid=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|qid=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds|1=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|qid=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult|1=Greek hero cult}} * {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|qid=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} * {{ill|Jinshōtai|ja|迅衝隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshōtai|1=Jinshōtai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638349}} * {{ill|Takechi Hanpeita|en|Takechi Hanpeita|qid=Q1074298|lt=Takechi Zuizan|1=Takechi Hanpeita}} * {{ill|Sakamoto Ryōma|en|Sakamoto Ryōma|qid=Q378450|lt=Sakamoto Ryoma|1=Sakamoto Ryōma}} * {{ill|Nakaoka Shintarō|en|Nakaoka Shintarō|qid=Q3138626|lt=Nakaoka Shintaro|1=Nakaoka Shintarō}} * {{ill|Yoshimura Torataro|ja|吉村虎太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimura Torataro|1=Yoshimura Toratarō|comment=changed title based on
